title: 📊 Sales Tax for US Freelancers Explained Simple
description:
Sales tax for US freelancers in 2026, who collects, when, and how. Plus the UK VAT registration threshold and quarterly returns. Practical step by step.

Personal finance, sales tax, freelancer taxes, VAT UK, sales tax 2026.

segments.voice: Sales tax in the United States is one of the most confusing areas of small business tax because there is no federal sales tax. Each state, county, and city sets its own rules. The Tax Foundation tracks more than thirteen thousand active sales tax jurisdictions across the country. Today, we untangle who collects, when, and how, including the post-Wayfair landscape.

Wayfair was the game-changer. South Dakota v Wayfair, decided by the Supreme Court in June twenty eighteen, ended the requirement that a seller have physical presence in a state before that state could require sales tax collection. Since then, every state has adopted economic nexus thresholds, typically based on revenue or transaction count.

Common state thresholds. Most states require sales tax registration after one hundred thousand dollars in sales or two hundred transactions in a calendar year. California is five hundred thousand. New York is five hundred thousand. The Streamlined Sales Tax Governing Board maintains the most current list at streamlinedsalestax dot org.

Services versus goods. Most US states do not tax services, only physical goods. So a freelance designer billing for design work usually has zero sales tax obligation regardless of state. A freelancer selling t-shirts or printed books is a different story, every shipment to a state where the seller has nexus may require sales tax collection.
segments.visual: services vs goods sales tax map

segments.voice: Some states do tax services. Hawaii, New Mexico, and South Dakota broadly tax services. Texas, Connecticut, and Iowa tax specific service categories like data processing or repair services. Always check your specific industry against your home state and any state where you do significant business. The state department of revenue website is the authoritative source.

Marketplace facilitator laws. If you sell on Amazon, eBay, Etsy, or similar platforms, the marketplace usually collects and remits sales tax for you. Per state laws passed since twenty nineteen, the marketplace facilitator is treated as the seller for sales tax purposes. So Etsy collects sales tax from the buyer and remits it directly to the state, no action required from you.

Where this gets complicated. If you sell on your own website using Shopify or WooCommerce, you are the seller, not the marketplace, and you handle sales tax yourself. Shopify Tax and TaxJar both calculate and file for you for around twenty to fifty dollars a month depending on volume. The cost is worth it the moment you cross nexus in two or more states.

Filing frequency. Once registered in a state, you file sales tax returns monthly, quarterly, or annually depending on your volume. Higher volume means more frequent filing. The state assigns a filing schedule when you register. Missing a filing, even with zero sales tax due, often triggers a small penalty per month plus interest.

Use tax, the part nobody talks about. If you buy supplies for your business from out of state without paying sales tax, you owe use tax to your home state. Use tax rates equal sales tax rates. This is heavily under-reported, but states are starting to enforce it during income tax audits, especially for businesses with high out-of-state vendor activity.

Drop shipping wrinkle. If you sell goods that ship directly from a supplier to your customer, the sales tax responsibility depends on which state the supplier ships from, where you have nexus, and where the customer is located. The Streamlined Sales Tax Project has specific drop shipping guidance, but most freelancers running drop ship businesses use software like Avalara to handle the complexity automatically.

Resale certificates and exemptions. If you buy goods to resell, you can use a resale certificate to avoid paying sales tax to your supplier. Each state has its own form, often called a resale exemption certificate. Sales Tax Institute publishes a guide. Many small businesses overpay sales tax on inventory purchases simply because they never set up a resale certificate.

The audit trigger. State sales tax auditors target businesses that have inconsistent reporting, businesses that crossed economic nexus thresholds and stopped filing, and industries known for cash transactions. The Multistate Tax Commission coordinates audit information across states. Once one state audits you, others sometimes follow.

Common sales tax mistakes. Not registering after crossing economic nexus, charging the wrong rate by mixing up state and local components, treating shipping and handling fees inconsistently, missing exemption certificate documentation. Each of these can balloon into thousands in back tax with penalties during an audit.

What to do if you missed registration. Most states have a Voluntary Disclosure Agreement program. If you come forward voluntarily, the state typically caps the lookback period to three years and waives penalties, in exchange for full payment of past sales tax owed. Compared to getting caught in an audit where the lookback can extend to seven years with full penalties, VDA is the cleaner path.

UK readers, VAT is your equivalent. The standard VAT rate in the UK is twenty percent. The registration threshold for two thousand twenty-six is ninety thousand pounds in taxable turnover over a rolling twelve-month period per HMRC guidance. Below that threshold, registration is voluntary. Above, mandatory within thirty days of crossing.

Voluntary VAT registration in the UK. Some businesses register voluntarily before hitting the threshold because it lets them reclaim VAT on business purchases. The trade-off is having to charge VAT to consumer customers, which can hurt competitiveness on consumer-facing services. Business-to-business sellers usually benefit from voluntary registration because their clients reclaim the VAT anyway.

Flat Rate VAT Scheme in the UK. If your turnover is under one hundred fifty thousand pounds excluding VAT, you can use the Flat Rate Scheme, which simplifies VAT by applying a flat percentage to your gross income, varying by industry. Limited cost businesses pay sixteen point five percent. Most service businesses pay between ten and fifteen percent, lower than full VAT calculation in many cases.
segments.visual: flat rate VAT scheme UK percentages

segments.voice: Quarterly VAT returns under MTD. Making Tax Digital for VAT, mandatory for all VAT-registered businesses since April twenty twenty-two, requires digital record keeping and submission via HMRC-recognized software. Xero, QuickBooks Online, and FreeAgent all handle MTD VAT submissions. The transition was rough for some businesses but the system is stable now.

EU sales for UK businesses post-Brexit. Selling to EU customers from the UK now triggers customs declarations and potentially VAT in the destination country. The IOSS scheme, Import One Stop Shop, lets you collect EU VAT at point of sale and remit through a single EU member state. For low-volume sellers, this complexity often pushes them toward EU-based fulfillment.

Reverse charge for B2B services. If you sell services to a VAT-registered business in another country, the reverse charge mechanism shifts VAT responsibility to the buyer. You issue an invoice with no VAT but reference the reverse charge rule. The buyer accounts for VAT in their country. This is the standard for most cross-border B2B service sales.

Practical sales tax software stack. For a US service freelancer, you probably need nothing beyond your accounting software. For a US goods seller, TaxJar at twenty-one dollars a month or Avalara at an enterprise tier handles multi-state filing. UK service freelancers below threshold need nothing. UK businesses above threshold need MTD-compliant accounting software, mandatory.

When to consult a state tax specialist. Once you cross nexus thresholds in three or more states, the complexity gets real. State tax specialists charge two to four hundred an hour for one-time consultations and routinely save businesses tens of thousands by structuring transactions optimally. The American Institute of CPAs has a directory of state tax specialists.

Documentation that survives an audit. Keep all invoices showing sales tax collected separately, all exemption certificates from buyers claiming exempt status, all records of out-of-state purchases that triggered use tax, all returns filed in each jurisdiction. Cloud storage organized by state and tax year saves you in the inevitable audit query down the road.

Subscribe to FinanzasClara and hit the bell. What's coming next week, the exact registration steps a Texas-based ecommerce seller used to clean up six states of missed nexus filings without triggering audits, plus the VDA template he used. Drop a comment with the state where you have the most sales tax confusion and I will tackle it.
